repositories.brief_summary: Active week with 10 merged PRs: GC fixes (ephemerons reverted+re-attempted), type system improvements, POWER CFI support, and testsuite speedups.
repositories.new_features_summary: POWER backend gets DWARF CFI support; modular explicits gain application warnings; class type system improved; Dynarray gets iarray conversion functions.
repositories.new_features:
- [#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14482): **DWARF CFI support for the POWER backend** merged, bringing backtraces and debugger/profiler support (GDB) to the last native backend that was missing them. Implemented by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist), reviewed by [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy).
- [#14667](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14667): **Application warnings for modular explicit (module-dependent) functions** — partial application, over-application, and unused argument warnings are now enabled for `Tfunctor` types. Implemented by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), reviewed by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche).
- [#14680](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14680): **Class type system improvements** (rebase of #12150 by [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25)) merged — fixes long-standing issues in the OO type system. Rebased and merged by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron).
- [#14394](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14394): **Refactor `type_let` into `type_let_nonrec` and `type_let_rec`** — a prerequisite for future improvements to recursive-binding typing. By [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob), reviewed by [Samuel Vivien](https://github.com/samsa1).
- [#14693](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14693): **`Dynarray.{of,to}_iarray` proposed** — adds conversions between `Dynarray` and `iarray` (immutable arrays) with a single-copy implementation. By [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole), approved by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche).
- [#14665](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14665): **Always emit `prefetchw` on x86/x86_64** — simplifies multi-domain performance tuning by removing the need for `-mprfchw` compiler flag. Merged. By [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA).

repositories.discussions_summary: Key debates: minor heap size defaults (performance), GC continuation finalisation semantics, extension points in AST for ppxlib compatibility.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Minor heap size defaults** ([#14634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14634)): [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) argues the 256KW (2MiB) default, unchanged since 2011, is too conservative for modern hardware. Benchmarks show up to 3x speedup with `s=32M` on allocation-heavy programs. [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) supports increasing the default (Jane Street has used 8MiB since 2017) but cautions against over-interpreting synthetic benchmarks.
- **GC for continuations** ([#14580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14580)): [KC Sivaramakrishnan](https://github.com/kayceesrk) proposes automatically finalising dropped effects continuations (resource cleanup when continuations are GC'd). A debate ensued about whether this encourages bad practice — `Fun.protect` finalizers becoming GC finalizers is unexpected. Only ~2% overhead on chameneos benchmark.
- **Extension points in the OCaml AST for ppxlib** ([#14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668)): [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) proposes adding extension point placeholders to more AST positions (especially `type_declaration`) so ppxlib can encode new language features without ppx breakage. The discussion explored concrete syntax proposals (`type [%foo ...]`) and a detailed analysis of AST node distances from existing extension points.
- **Ephemeron race condition** ([#14679](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14679)): [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) diagnosed a race in #14657 where `num_domains_to_mark` was decremented before `ephe_next_round()` was called, causing the major GC to deadlock in multi-domain programs. The PR was reverted (#14682) and a fixed version (#14688) proposed.
- **`keep-expansion` type-checker PR** ([#14673](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14673)): Ecosystem testing of #11648 (Jacques Garrigue's multi-year type-checker correctness fix) revealed two bugs: an "Unbound record field" in ppxlib and a `Ctype.Unify` exception in base_quickcheck. Detailed type-level debugging of level/scope invariant violations with `Texpand` nodes.
- **Segfaults in cleanup-at-exit mode** ([#14594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14594)): Continued debate on whether `caml_shutdown` should try to cancel unjoined domains. [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) argues the cleanup-at-exit mode has both false positives and false negatives and should be deleted; [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) proposes making `caml_shutdown` clean up global-variable state.
repositories.trends_summary: Strong focus on GC correctness (ephemerons, continuations), type-checker infrastructure refactoring, and closing long-standing backend gaps (POWER CFI).
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Ephemeron performance and correctness**: The week saw a complete cycle of merge, revert, and re-attempt of ephemeron GC optimization (#14657 → #14682 → #14688), demonstrating the difficulty of concurrent GC modifications. The eventual fix targets a quadratic blowup affecting Rocq proof assistant's evar map use case.
- **Type-checker infrastructure overhaul**: Multiple interrelated PRs (#14394, #14538, #14517) by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) systematically refactor `type_let` and `type_approx` to enable correct handling of polymorphic recursive bindings — groundwork for fixing #14253.
- **`keep-expansion` type correctness** (#11648/#14673): [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue)'s multi-year effort to fix principality and non-termination bugs via `Texpand` type nodes is approaching merger, with ecosystem testing now revealing integration issues in ppxlib and base_quickcheck.
- **CI robustness and testsuite speed**: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) merged multiple PRs reducing testsuite runtime (lazy5.ml: 10s→0.1s, memory-model tests: 7s→2s) and fixing flaky tests.
- **Architecture backend completeness**: POWER CFI support completes native debugger support across all supported architectures; s390x CFI fix (#14500) is also pending.
- **ppxlib/compiler interface evolution**: Discussion on adding extension points to more AST positions reflects ongoing coordination between ppxlib (moving to a frozen AST model) and the compiler team.

repositories.brief_summary: Week focused on open PRs addressing repository loading bugs, symlink handling, Nushell support, and disk space optimization.
repositories.new_features_summary: A new PR proposes adding Nushell shell support for opam env via JSON output.
repositories.new_features:
- **Nushell shell support** via JSON-based environment loading (`opam env | to json`) opened by [@jneem](https://github.com/jneem), continuing work from [#6330](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6330) ([#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6873))
- **Symlink transparency in `get_diff`**: PR by [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto) proposes replacing `Unix.lstat` with `Unix.stat` so `file://` repositories containing symlinks no longer fail with "Symlinks are unsupported" ([#6874](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6874))
repositories.activity_summary: Four PRs were open during the week covering repository loading, shell portability, and file handling improvements.
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened**: [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto) opened [#6874](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6874) to follow symlinks transparently in `get_diff`, fixing failures when using `file://` repositories with symlinks
- **Newly opened**: [@jneem](https://github.com/jneem) opened [#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6873) adding Nushell shell support for `opam env`
- **Ongoing**: [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) has [#6871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6871) (draft) open to update the repository diff-loading mechanism, simplifying code and preventing updates to unrelated paths
- **Ongoing**: [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) has [#6440](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6440) open to reduce disk usage by caching only dev packages into the `.opam-switch/sources` directory
- **Issue opened**: [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) filed [#6872](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6872) reporting that incremental repository loading introduced in opam 2.5.0 scans from the repository root rather than the `packages/` subdirectory, causing missed packages on first load
- **Issue active**: [John Whitington](https://github.com/johnwhitington) reported [#6870](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6870) where `opam publish` uses a wrong archive URL when run automatically after the plugin is installed
repositories.discussions_summary: Active review threads on Nushell env quoting semantics and symlink handling design intent occupied most discussion this week.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- On [#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6873), [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) and [@jneem](https://github.com/jneem) worked through Nushell-specific quoting behaviour, the correct default config path per platform (Linux vs macOS/Windows), and the handling of boolean environment values across Nushell versions
- On [#6874](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6874), [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) questioned whether symlink support should be added at all given intentional earlier restrictions, asking why setup-ocaml uses symlinks for package aliases rather than copies
- On [#6871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6871), [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) and [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) discussed how to correctly identify opam file paths within arbitrarily nested `packages/` subdirectories during diff-based loading
repositories.trends_summary: Shell portability and repository reliability across file-based and incremental backends are recurring themes this week.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Shell portability continues to expand beyond POSIX/fish/zsh, with Nushell joining PowerShell as an explicitly supported target ([#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6873))
- Incremental repository loading introduced in opam 2.5.0 is surfacing edge cases: both [#6871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6871) and [#6872](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6872) address incorrect path filtering during diff-based updates
- The `file://` repository backend is getting scrutiny around its symlink and diff behaviour ([#6874](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6874), [#6410](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6410))

repositories.brief_summary: Revert of signature_help readability changes opened after discovering editors ignore line breaks in label fields.
repositories.activity_summary: One PR opened to revert a previous signature_help improvement, plus an automated Nix flake.lock dependency update.
repositories.activity:
- [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) opened [#1598](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1598) to **revert the `signature_help` readability improvements** from [#1580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1580), after discovering that editors render the signature label field as a single line and ignore any introduced line breaks, making the formatting changes ineffective; the plan is to find an LSP-compatible approach before reintroducing the feature
- An automated **Nix flake.lock dependency update** ([#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437)) remains open, bumping `merlin5_3` to a March 2026 snapshot and updating `nixpkgs` and `nix-overlays` inputs
repositories.trends_summary: Editor compatibility constraints are shaping LSP feature design, with line-break formatting found to be unsupported in signature label fields.
repositories.emerging_trends: - The revert in [#1598](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1598) highlights an **LSP editor-compatibility constraint**: signature label fields are rendered as single lines by editors such as VS Code and Emacs, meaning multi-line formatting improvements must find alternative representation strategies before landing
repositories.contributors_summary: Charlène_Gros opened a revert PR for signature_help; automated tooling updated the Nix flake lock file.
repositories.contributors:

repositories.brief_summary: Indexer improvements in progress: LRU cache for memory and pointer-based index files; ocamlformat 0.29.0 upgrade merged.
repositories.activity_summary: Two indexer feature PRs opened alongside a code-formatting upgrade merge and routine CI dependency bumps.
repositories.activity:
- **LRU cache for the indexer** opened by [Charlène Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) ([#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)): replaces the unbounded `Hashtable` in the indexer with an LRU cache to bound memory usage, with active review discussion around implementation details including initial hashtable sizing, doubly-linked list design, and memory-footprint-aware eviction
- **Pointing index support for ocaml-index** opened by [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d) ([#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)): allows an index file to reference another existing index on disk, resulting in smaller index files and fewer write operations; reviewers noted concerns about stale pointer targets and shared cache correctness
- **Emacs CI action** bump from 6.0 to 8.0 opened by [Dependabot](https://github.com/dependabot) ([#2049](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2049)), updating the `purcell/setup-emacs` action used in CI workflows
- **ocamlformat 0.29.0 upgrade** merged by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) ([#2047](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2047)), reformatting 60 source files across the entire codebase with the new formatter version
- Long-standing feature request for **LSIF (Language Server Index Format) support** ([#1017](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1017)), originally opened by [Chris Wendt](https://github.com/chrismwendt) in 2019, was closed this week
repositories.discussions_summary: Active design discussion on LRU cache implementation and correctness of pointer-based index files.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **LRU implementation design** in [#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050) covered trade-offs between pre-allocated arrays (as used in `cachecache`) versus dynamic allocation, appropriate initial hashtable sizes, and whether memory-size-weighted eviction is needed; [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w), [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw), and [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) all commented on correctness and performance considerations
- **Safety concerns for pointing indexes** in [#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051): [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) raised the risk of a pointing index targeting a removed or updated file, and noted that multiple pointing indexes sharing a target could end up with separate caches instead of a shared one, suggesting `File_cache` as the right abstraction
repositories.trends_summary: Ongoing focus on reducing the memory footprint and storage overhead of the ocaml-index component.
repositories.emerging_trends: - Both new PRs this week ([#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050), [#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)) target the **indexer subsystem**, indicating a focused effort to improve its memory usage and on-disk efficiency through bounded caching and reference-based index files

repositories.brief_summary: Directory diffing, promote prefix support, and sandboxed corrections merged alongside 3.22.1 release prep and OCaml 4.14 minimum bump.
repositories.new_features_summary: Directory diffing, promote prefix targeting, sandboxed corrections, `dune clean` path args, and `--debug-backtraces` for promote/trace/cache.
repositories.new_features:
- **Directory diffing** in `diff` actions: the `diff` stanza now supports diffing entire directories, resolving a long-standing feature request (open since 2018). Requires `(lang dune 3.23)`. ([#13880](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13880), closes [#3567](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/3567))
- **Promote prefix targeting**: `dune promote` now promotes all paths that share the given path as a prefix, enabling selective bulk promotion of related files. ([#13876](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13876))
- **Sandboxed rule corrections**: sandboxed actions can now produce promotable corrected files when `(corrections produce)` is set; `.corrected` files are auto-registered. ([#13813](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13813))
- **`dune clean` path arguments**: `dune clean` now accepts path arguments to remove specific targets from `_build` rather than cleaning the entire directory. ([#13875](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13875))
- **`--debug-backtraces` flag** added to `dune promote`, `dune cache`, and `dune trace` subcommands for debugging. ([#13933](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13933))
- **Dirs stanza hint**: dune now emits a helpful hint when attempting to build a target excluded by a `(dirs ...)` stanza. ([#13919](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13919))
- **Minimum OCaml version raised to 4.14**: the minimum supported OCaml version has been bumped from 4.08 to 4.14 across all dune packages, enabling removal of long-accumulated compatibility shims. ([#13533](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13533))
- **Improved opam variable translation errors**: error messages for unsupported opam variable translations in package management are now clearer and more actionable. ([#13954](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13954))
- Fixed `dune pkg lock` to **prefer stronger hashes** (SHA256/SHA512 over MD5) when multiple hash options are available. ([#13901](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13901), closes [#13818](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13818))
- Fixed `dune pkg lock` to **allow `file://` URLs with relative paths outside the workspace**. ([#13915](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13915), closes [#10254](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/10254))
- Fixed **`enabled_if` evaluation in library entries** scoped to a package, resolving a dependency cycle. ([#13833](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13833))
- Fixed `dune trace cat --chrome-trace` to use **microsecond granularity** timestamps instead of nanoseconds. ([#13911](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13911), closes [#13906](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13906))
- Fixed **`.exe` extension handling** in `.install` files on Windows for package management. ([#13864](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13864), closes [#12430](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12430))
- Fixed **Windows 7 compatibility** in `time_stubs.c` bootstrap by adding a fallback for `GetSystemTimePreciseAsFileTime`. ([#13905](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13905), closes [#13871](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13871))
- Fixed **`OPAMSWITCH` environment variable** not being set in package build environments. ([#13888](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13888), closes [#13887](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13887))
repositories.activity_summary: Active week with new diff/promote capabilities merged, extensive internal refactoring, 3.22.1 backports, and several new PRs targeting upcoming features.
repositories.activity:

repositories.discussions_summary: OxCaml CI integration proposed, command retry when global lock held requested, and RPC error message improvement filed as good first issue.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) reported a regression in 3.22.0 affecting OxCaml builds ([#13889](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13889)) and opened a proposal for **OxCaml CI testing integration** ([#13895](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13895)), prompting team discussion about adding OxCaml to the CI matrix
- [Mathieu Barbin](https://github.com/mbarbin) raised the question of **building toplevel targets selectively** — only when within a package or under an enabling condition ([#13920](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13920)), touching on user-facing build scope control
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) proposed **command retry support when the global lock is held** ([#13964](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13964)), improving UX for concurrent dune invocations
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) proposed running a key subset of **reverse dependency checks in CI** as part of the release process ([#13897](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13897)), as part of release hardening efforts
- [Benjamin Somers](https://github.com/bensmrs) filed [#13894](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13894) investigating **interactions between `%{version:...}` and OPAM dependencies** where version variables may not resolve correctly
repositories.trends_summary: Systematic expansion of diff/promote capabilities, internal representation refactoring, and steady package management hardening across Windows edge cases.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Expanding `diff` and `promote` capabilities**: multiple PRs extended what dune can diff and promote — directories ([#13880](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13880)), path prefixes ([#13876](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13876)), and sandboxed corrections ([#13813](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13813)) — suggesting a deliberate push to make the diff/promote workflow more powerful and flexible
- **Internal representation and digest refactoring**: a new `Stdune.Repr` generic runtime representation module ([#13938](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13938)) and extensive digest module decomposition ([#13956](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13956), [#13957](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13957), [#13958](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13958)) indicate a longer-term effort to improve dune's internal data model and serialization
- **3.22.1 maintenance release** in active preparation with [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) coordinating four backport merges and the release draft ([#13947](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13947)), targeting mainly Windows and portability fixes
- **OCaml 4.14 minimum version** established, enabling removal of years of accumulated compatibility shims across boot, stdune, and other libraries ([#13533](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13533), [#13924](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13924), [#13925](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13925), [#13926](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13926), [#13927](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13927))
- **Package management robustness**: ongoing effort to fix edge cases in `dune pkg` — Windows executable handling ([#13864](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13864)), OPAM environment integration ([#13888](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13888)), hash strength ([#13901](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13901)), lock path resolution ([#13915](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13915))
repositories.issues_summary: One good first issue open: improve the 'RPC server not running' error message for better user experience.
repositories.good_first_issues: - [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) filed [#13902](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13902) requesting an **improved error message when the RPC server is not running** — currently shows a raw internal error; a good opportunity to improve user-facing error messages in the RPC subsystem with a targeted, focused change

repositories.brief_summary: OCaml 5.5 compatibility draft PR opened; new issue tracking atomic field annotations; ongoing OxCaml fix in review.
repositories.activity_summary: Draft PRs advancing OCaml 5.5 and OxCaml support, with a new issue requesting display of atomic record field annotations.
repositories.activity:
- **OCaml 5.5 compatibility** draft PR opened by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd): [#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406) adds support for external types (displayed as abstract types) and module arrows introduced in OCaml 5.5; path references to module arguments currently show as unresolved links, noted as a known limitation to be addressed before merging
- **OxCaml polymorphic parameter parentheses** fix remains open as a draft by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) in [#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1404), awaiting merge of [#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399); covers rendering of polymorphic parameters (also upstreamed to ocaml/ocaml as [ocaml/ocaml#13806](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13806) and expected in OCaml 5.5)
- **New issue [#1405](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1405)** filed by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) requesting that `[@atomic]` annotations on mutable record fields (available since OCaml 5.4) be reflected in odoc's rendered output
- **OCaml 5.5 tracking issue [#1401](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1401)** updated with confirmation that [#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406) is in progress and has been added to opam-alpha-repository
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on PR #1317 raised concerns about ocamlformat 0.29 changing code block indentation behavior in odoc comments.
repositories.notable_discussions: - Ongoing discussion in the long-merged [#1317](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1317) (code block "box model" fix): [Mathieu Barbin](https://github.com/mbarbin) reported that upgrading to `ocamlformat 0.29.0` caused widespread indentation changes in odoc-style code blocks across their codebase, creating a conflict with `ocp-indent`; [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) noted the limitation that fully left-trimming the first line would prevent intentionally indented code examples; [Jules Aguillon](https://github.com/Julow) suggested a potential `{@ocaml noformat[ ... ]}` syntax to allow code blocks to opt out of ocamlformat reformatting
repositories.trends_summary: Active effort to keep odoc compatible with upcoming OCaml 5.5 features, including both upstream OCaml and OxCaml extensions.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **OCaml 5.5 readiness** is a clear focus, with both a dedicated compat PR [#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406) and a tracking issue [#1401](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1401); the new language features (external types, module arrows, atomic record fields) each require distinct handling in odoc's type rendering and documentation model
- **OxCaml/upstream alignment**: polymorphic parameter support being worked on in [#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1404) covers a feature present in both OxCaml and the upcoming OCaml 5.5, reflecting an effort to track Jane Street extensions as they move upstream
repositories.contributors_summary: Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac drove most activity, opening a new PR and issue; Kate and art-w contributed review feedback.
repositories.contributors:
- [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) opened the draft OCaml 5.5 compat PR [#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406) and filed issue [#1405](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1405) requesting `[@atomic]` annotation display; also commented on the OCaml 5.5 tracking issue [#1401](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1401)
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) reviewed [#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406) with a suggestion to tighten the opam version constraint to `< "5.6"`, and confirmed the PR was added to opam-alpha-repository via [#1401](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1401)
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) maintains the open draft PR [#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1404) fixing parenthesisation of OxCaml polymorphic parameters
- [Mathieu Barbin](https://github.com/mbarbin) raised a user-facing concern in [#1317](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1317) about ocamlformat 0.29 altering code block indentation in odoc comments
- [Jules Aguillon](https://github.com/Julow) responded in [#1317](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1317) with context on the ocamlformat behavior and proposed a possible `noformat` code block attribute

repositories.brief_summary: OCaml 5.4.1 and Dune 3.22.0 release data added; five new tutorials opened for review.
repositories.new_features_summary: OCaml 5.4.1 release page, Dune 3.22.0 announcement, and updated opam switch docs added to the site.
repositories.new_features:
- **OCaml 5.4.1 release data** added to the site, covering security fix OSEC-2026-01, TSan fixes, a macOS `-pack` fix, and a string miscompilation fix; the release is now marked `is_latest` ([#3576](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3576) by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac))
- **Dune 3.22.0 release announcement** published with a full 248-line changelog covering new tracing features, OxCaml support, and multiple bug fixes ([#3570](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3570) by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder))
- **Updated opam switch tutorial** (`getting-started/2_02_opam_switch.md`) to reference OCaml 5.4.1 and adds a note to run `opam update` if the newest version is absent from the list ([#3574](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3574) by [Penelope Yong](https://github.com/penelopeysm))
- **Fixed rendering of `direnv` steps** in the opam-path tutorial (`platform/0_09_opam_path.md`) by correcting indentation of code blocks inside numbered list items ([#3574](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3574) by [Penelope Yong](https://github.com/penelopeysm))
- **Markdown lint corrections** for `4.14.1` and `4.14.2` release data (heading style, code block language tags, list formatting) ([#3572](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3572) by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac))
repositories.activity_summary: Five new tutorials and an llms.txt page opened by Cuihtlauac; automated planet scrapes brought in eight new blog posts.
repositories.activity:

repositories.brief_summary: CI tooling maintenance week with Biome linter update and two new Wasm correctness fixes opened.
repositories.new_features_summary: JS runtime uses optional chaining for more robust null checks; variable coalescing pass deferred from release.
repositories.new_features:
- The **JS runtime** was updated to use optional chaining (`?.`) in null-safety checks within `caml_wrap_exception` and `caml_current_dir`, making stack overflow detection and directory resolution more concise ([189a48a](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/189a48a5a86581d2ef69f54e29d9be9260043092), [#2202](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2202))
- The **variable coalescing pass** (`#2166`) was removed from the upcoming release changelog, indicating that feature has been deferred ([7d0de8f](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/7d0de8f3ee5dd2b2ba8610aa35e7b6a22eab9610))
repositories.activity_summary: Two new Wasm correctness PRs opened; ongoing large PRs for Dynlink support and OCaml 5.5 compatibility remain active.
repositories.activity:
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) opened [#2207](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2207) to fix **Wasm empty closure identity**: empty closures were all physically equal due to sharing a `(ref.i31 (i32.const 0))` placeholder, breaking `(==)` comparisons between distinct closures; the fix allocates a distinct global `struct.new` for each empty closure
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) opened [#2208](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2208) to **guard Wasm direct calls against stale global data flow data**, preventing miscompilation when `Global_flow.get_unique_closure` returns a closure variable that was subsequently eliminated by dead code analysis
- The large **Dynlink / toplevel support** PR [#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) received activity from [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo), who contributed additions and requested review; the PR touches 140 files with 6362 additions
- The draft **OCaml 5.5 initial support** PR [#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) (72 files, 11865 additions) was updated, remaining in draft state
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) updated **Biome to 2.4.8** and fixed associated lint warnings across the JS runtime and configuration files ([#2202](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2202))
- A series of **Windows CI fixes** were attempted and then reverted: dune was pinned to 3.21.0 to work around a cram test regression in 3.22.0 where `/cygdrive/` paths caused all commands to output "UNREACHABLE" ([#2203](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2203)), but the fix was ineffective and reverted ([#2204](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2204)); similarly, `setup-ocaml` was pinned to v3.4.8 ([#2205](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2205)) and then reverted ([#2206](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2206))
repositories.discussions_summary: Ali Caglayan flagged that the Windows CI dune pin did not resolve the cram test failures.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) noted in [#2203](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2203) that pinning dune 3.21.0 on Windows did not fix the CI regression, prompting [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) to revert and try the `setup-ocaml` pin instead
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) noted uncertainty about how to reproduce the stale global data flow issue in [#2208](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2208), which may affect review and testing
repositories.trends_summary: Continued focus on Wasm correctness with two new targeted fixes, alongside ongoing large-scope Wasm feature PRs.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Wasm backend correctness** remains an active area, with [#2207](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2207) and [#2208](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2208) both addressing subtle runtime semantics issues specific to the Wasm compilation target
- **Windows CI stability** is proving difficult: two separate approaches (pinning dune and pinning setup-ocaml) were attempted and reverted within the same day, suggesting the underlying cram test path issue on Cygwin remains unresolved
- The **Dynlink / toplevel** work in [#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187) and **OCaml 5.5 support** in [#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195) represent two large-scale efforts in progress that will substantially expand runtime capabilities
repositories.contributors_summary: hhugo drove all merged CI/tooling work; Jérôme Vouillon opened two Wasm bug fix PRs and is primary author of the Dynlink PR.
repositories.contributors:
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) merged all five PRs this week: Biome update ([#2202](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2202)), Windows CI dune pin and revert ([#2203](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2203), [#2204](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2204)), and setup-ocaml pin and revert ([#2205](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2205), [#2206](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2206)); also contributed code to the Dynlink PR [#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187) and maintains the draft OCaml 5.5 PR [#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195)
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) opened two new Wasm correctness PRs ([#2207](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2207), [#2208](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2208)) and is the primary author of the large Dynlink / toplevel support PR [#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187)

repositories.brief_summary: Active week with 33 merges spanning metaprogramming soundness, mode system renames, ARM64 improvements, and multiple reaper bug fixes.
repositories.new_features_summary: New Gc.Memprof API, metaprogramming compiler flags, sound quotes/splices kinding, and mode annotations under quotes.
repositories.new_features:
- **`Gc.Memprof.participate_globally`** added to the standard library, enabling memory profiling participation across all domains; the existing `participate` function was renamed to `enlist` and the new function surfaces as `enlist_all_domains` in the public API ([#5644](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5644))
- **`~level` parameter removed** from the jkind comparison and violation API (`Jkind.compare`, `Jkind.check_sub`, etc.), simplifying call sites throughout the type checker ([#5615](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5615))
- **Sound kinding of quotes and splices** — the metaprogramming system now enforces correct kinding rules at quote and splice boundaries, with `camlinternalQuote.mli` gaining a new `Modes` module (`legacy`, `of_string_list`) and `Type.arrow` accepting two extra `Modes.t` arguments ([#5636](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5636))
- **Mode annotations under quotes** are now supported, allowing programmers to annotate modes inside quoted expressions; related changes in `Pat.constraint_` and `Type_constraint.constraint_` in `camlinternalQuote.mli` ([#5670](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5670))
- **External modalities in `with`-bounds** — the type system now supports external modality constraints in `with`-bound expressions, expanding the expressiveness of the modes system ([#5601](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5601))
- **New compiler flags `-requires-metaprogramming` and `-uses-metaprogramming`** added to `driver/main_args.mli`, along with a new `ui_requires_metaprogramming` field in `cmx_format.mli` unit info structures, enabling the compiler to track and enforce metaprogramming dependencies ([#5544](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5544))
- **`eval` made a regular function** — the metaprogramming `eval` extension point was replaced with a standard function call, removing the special-case extension point syntax and simplifying the implementation across 66 files ([#5657](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5657))
- **`observing` mode renamed to `reading`** across the codebase and documentation (including `jane/doc/extensions/_05-modes/intro.md`), aligning the mode name with its intended semantics ([#5712](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5712))
- **ARM64 instruction sizes derived robustly** — a comprehensive rework (`backend/branch_relaxation_intf.mli`, `backend/arm64/ast/ast.mli`) replaces fragile hardcoded sizes with derived measurements; `Instruction.max_displacement` added; `with_measuring` now returns a `measurement` record; `relax_poll`, `relax_allocation`, `relax_condbranch`, `relax_branch` separated as distinct functions ([#5499](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5499))
- **x86 LEA instruction used for add/sub with immediates** — the x86 backend now emits `lea` for additions and subtractions with immediate operands, improving code generation quality ([#5695](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5695))
- **Register allocation interference edges** now added between multiple outputs of the same instruction, preventing incorrect register assignments for instructions that produce multiple results ([#5706](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5706))
- **CFG merge block stale predecessors fixed** — `backend/cfg/cfg.mli` gains a correction so that predecessor sets for merge blocks are correctly maintained after block insertion ([#5708](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5708))
- **`Multicore` module now keeps domains alive** — fixed a latency issue where joining domains could block indefinitely waiting for the ticker thread; `domain.mli` alert text updated to warn about using both `Domain.spawn` and `Multicore` together ([#5638](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5638))
- **Jkind error message formatting fixed** — extra blank line removed from jkind error output, producing cleaner compiler diagnostics ([#5578](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5578))
- **Safely errors on jkind annotations in quotes** — instead of silently dropping or mishandling jkind annotations inside quoted expressions, the compiler now raises a descriptive error ([#5685](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5685))
repositories.activity_summary: Dozens of PRs opened spanning metaprogramming staging, new modes, codegen improvements, and continued 5.4 upstream merge work.
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened metaprogramming PRs** this week extend quote/splice soundness: sound moding of quotes and splices by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) ([#5697](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5697)), quotes of expansive expressions at mode `once` ([#5698](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5698)), top-level expressions at mode `once` ([#5715](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5715)), and environment stage tracking in typing with GADT interaction tests ([#5641](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5641))
- **New mode system PRs opened**: `write` and `writing` modes by [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) ([#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608)), `corrupted` and `corruptable` modes ([#5713](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5713)), `poisoned` and `poisoning` modes (draft, [#5666](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5666)), and `unique implies uncontended` by [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) ([#5716](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5716))
- **New codegen and compiler infrastructure PRs opened**: CFG SSA reconstruction by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) ([#5722](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5722)), codegen examples for materialized bits ([#5727](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5727)), new Lfor compilation strategy by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) ([#5728](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5728)), flag `-thunkify-compilation-unit-initialization` by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) ([#5720](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5720)), and static modules by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) ([#5724](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5724))
- **5.4 upstream merge work** continues with multiple ongoing and newly-opened PRs: towards labeled tuples support ([#5692](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5692)), Stdlib iarray ([#5693](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5693)), towards `make install` for OxCaml 5.4 ([#5717](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5717)), testsuite conflicts ([#5711](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5711)), and runtime merge ([#5598](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5598)) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) and [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes); completed merges include stdlib conflicts ([#5616](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5616)), ocamltest ([#5605](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5605)), debugger ([#5617](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5617)), and miscellany ([#5729](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5729))
- **Reaper correctness cluster** completed: three fixes merged for the Flambda2 reaper — exception parameter deletion bug ([#5563](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5563)), deadlock between uses and sources computation with local fields ([#5648](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5648)), and suppression of value slot stamp printing without the stamps debug flag ([#5700](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5700)), all by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs); also a reaper testcase for [#5633](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5633) ([#5682](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5682)) and coaccessor/coconstructor renames ([#5681](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5681))
- **Flambda2 continuation lifting correctness** improved with a fix preventing lifting of continuations that may refer to non-lifted continuations by [Guillaume Bury](https://github.com/Gbury) ([#5689](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5689)), and a fix for unbound variables in lifted constants by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) ([#5725](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5725)), superseding the earlier approach in [#5719](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5719)
- **n-way join improvements** merged: missing-cmx variable scoping fix by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) ([#5679](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5679)), with a follow-on PR using untagged const false for nullability detection ([#5718](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5718)) opened; related issue [#5721](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5721) opened reporting missing CSE with n-way join
- **Sort and jkind type system cleanup**: generic sort variable simplification by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) ([#5704](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5704)), `Jkind.Sort.new_var` return type corrected to `Jkind.Sort.var` ([#5658](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5658)), and parameter vs return mode fix ([#5596](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5596)); ongoing: `moregeneral` should regeneralize layout vars on error ([#5691](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5691)), `Texp_apply_layout` for layout-poly instantiation ([#5634](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5634)), `let poly_` type checking ([#5513](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5513))
- **Slambda fracturing** initial implementation merged — adds the simplest slambda fracturing pass by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) ([#5544](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5544)), laying groundwork for more advanced code splitting
- **`iarrayLabels.ml` sync** — fixes propagated from `iarray.ml` to the labeled variant by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) ([#5683](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5683)); ongoing Stdlib iarray 5.4 merge work in ([#5693](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5693))
- **CI and build fixes**: dune pinned to 3.20.2 in CI ([#5684](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5684)), Dune 3.22 compilation compatibility fix ([#5686](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5686)), missing compiler-libs-installation.sexp entries added ([#5709](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5709)), macOS compilation fix opened by [Amethyst Shen](https://github.com/Amekagura) ([#5714](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5714))
- **Ongoing abstract kinds** work by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin): `with kind_` constraints (part 3, [#5629](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5629)) and tests for `is_really_poly` in typecore ([#5730](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5730)); also ongoing zero-alloc argument support ([#5493](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5493))
- **Ongoing mode polymorphism** work by [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges): functional mode polymorphism ([#5483](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5483)), alloc mode ([#5482](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5482)), copy operation ([#5481](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5481)), normal form morphisms ([#5453](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5453)), and printing of polymorphic mode variables ([#5488](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5488))
- **JIT and Opttoploop fixes** for mdx integration opened by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) ([#5710](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5710)); statmemprof comment improvements by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) ([#5707](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5707)); `custom or_nulls` by [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) ([#5668](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5668))
- **`unsafe_multidomain` and `do_not_spawn_domains` alerts** turned off in the test suite by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) ([#5694](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5694)), touching 108 test files
- **Allow immediates in upstream-compatible code** opened by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) ([#5699](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5699)); `Typedtree` constants for unboxed unit and bool by [Luke Maurer](https://github.com/lukemaurer) ([#5690](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5690)); `my_region/my_ghost_region` parameter merge by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) ([#5702](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5702))
repositories.discussions_summary: Deep reviews on zero-alloc arguments and mode lattice theory; active debate on preemption stack traversal and unique-implies-uncontended semantics.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Zero-alloc function arguments** ([#5493](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5493)) received the most review activity this week — [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) posted extensive comments covering `Tpoly` node handling, `is_really_poly` correctness, `filter_arrow` interactions, the distinction between `Zero_alloc.check option` and `Zero_alloc.t`, and `translcore` complications; the PR by [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian) remains open pending resolution
- **`unique implies uncontended`** ([#5716](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5716)) prompted theoretical discussion between [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) and [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25) on lattice structure for the modes system — specifically whether `aliased => contended` should be modeled as a general mechanism, how normal forms interact with fixpoints in mode operators, and whether this belongs as a jkind axis
- **Tick thread in runtime** ([#5349](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5349)) continued review by [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) covering threading model correctness, non-blocking syscall safety, locking discipline, and interval timer accuracy for preemptive scheduling
- **Preemption** ([#4881](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4881)) saw discussion between [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) and [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) on GC-safe stack frame traversal during preemption; notably the PR description mentions its test suite was written by Claude
- **`raise` instead of dropping jkind annotations in quotes** ([#5674](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5674)) discussion about how the compiler should handle jkind annotations encountered inside quoted expressions, leading to the merged fix in ([#5685](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5685))
repositories.trends_summary: Metaprogramming (quotes/splices) soundness work is accelerating alongside steady expansion of the modes system with new named modes.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Metaprogramming soundness hardening** is a clear focus: four PRs merged this week fixing quote/splice kinding ([#5636](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5636)), mode annotations in quotes ([#5670](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5670)), `eval` semantics ([#5657](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5657)), and jkind annotation safety ([#5685](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5685)), with four more PRs opened extending the same area ([#5697](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5697), [#5698](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5698), [#5715](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5715), [#5641](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5641))
- **Mode system vocabulary expansion**: three new mode pairs introduced this week — `write`/`writing` ([#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608)), `corrupted`/`corruptable` ([#5713](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5713)), `poisoned`/`poisoning` ([#5666](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5666)) — on top of the `observing`→`reading` rename that merged; the mode lattice is actively evolving
- **Backend code quality improvements** show sustained attention: ARM64 instruction size derivation ([#5499](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5499)), x86 LEA usage ([#5695](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5695)), register allocation interference ([#5706](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5706)), x86 peephole optimizations ongoing ([#5639](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5639)), push/pop spilling ([#5635](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5635)), CFG SSA reconstruction ([#5722](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5722)), and optimization fuel bisection ([#5651](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5651))
- **Flambda2 reaper reliability** is being systematically improved: three fixes in one week ([#5563](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5563), [#5648](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5648), [#5700](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5700)) alongside continuation lifting fixes ([#5689](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5689), [#5725](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5725)), suggesting the reaper is in active hardening phase
- **5.4 upstream merge** is in sustained progress with dedicated contributors ([David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js), [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon), [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes)) working through stdlib, runtime, toplevel, ocamltest, and debugger subsystems in parallel ([#5692](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5692), [#5693](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5693), [#5598](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5598), [#5711](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5711), [#5717](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5717))

repositories.brief_summary: Discussion continued on a race condition bug in Eio.Path.load that also prevents reading /proc files.
repositories.activity_summary: Ongoing discussion of a race condition in Eio.Path.load affecting /proc filesystem reads.
repositories.activity: - The open issue [#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835) on a **race condition in `Eio.Path.load`** received a follow-up comment during the week linking background reading on `/proc` file behavior, keeping the discussion active ahead of a potential fix.
repositories.discussions_summary: Race condition in Eio.Path.load causing silent data loss and failures when reading /proc files.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) filed [#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835) describing how **`Eio.Path.load` uses file size as an allocation hint** before reading, introducing a race condition: if the file shrinks between `stat` and `read`, the function silently returns truncated data; if it grows, data is lost. The issue also affects `/proc` virtual files, which report size 0 at `stat` time and thus return empty strings when read via `Eio.Path.load`. The proposed fix is to treat the stat size only as a hint for initial buffer sizing and otherwise behave like `Eio.Path.with_open_in path Eio.Flow.read_all`.
repositories.contributors_summary: Volker Diels-Grabsch reported and provided context on the Eio.Path.load race condition issue.
repositories.contributors: - [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) reported the `Eio.Path.load` race condition ([#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835)), provided a reproducer showing `/proc/sys/fs/file-nr` returning an empty string, proposed a concrete fix strategy, and added a follow-up comment with background reading on `/proc` file semantics.

group_summaries.brief_summary: dune advances diff/promote features and prepares 3.22.1 release; merlin, odoc, opam, js_of_ocaml, and ocaml-lsp see incremental improvements.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: dune gains directory diffing, promote prefix, sandboxed corrections, and clean path args; opam adds Nushell shell support.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **dune: Directory diffing** in `diff` actions ([ocaml/dune#13880](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13880)): the `diff` stanza now supports diffing entire directories (requires `lang dune 3.23`), closing a request open since 2018 ([ocaml/dune#3567](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/3567))
- **dune: Promote prefix targeting** ([ocaml/dune#13876](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13876)): `dune promote` can now promote all paths sharing a given prefix, enabling selective bulk promotion
- **dune: Sandboxed rule corrections** ([ocaml/dune#13813](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13813)): sandboxed actions can produce promotable `.corrected` files when `(corrections produce)` is set
- **dune: `dune clean` path arguments** ([ocaml/dune#13875](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13875)): `dune clean` now accepts path arguments to remove specific targets from `_build`
- **dune: `--debug-backtraces` flag** added to `dune promote`, `dune cache`, and `dune trace` subcommands ([ocaml/dune#13933](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13933))
- **dune: Minimum OCaml version raised to 4.14** ([ocaml/dune#13533](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13533)): drops accumulated compatibility shims for `Either`, `Val_none`, `Array.find_opt`, `String`, and boot stdlib modules
- **opam: Nushell shell support** via JSON-based environment loading (`opam env | to json`) opened by [@jneem](https://github.com/jneem) ([ocaml/opam#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6873))
- **opam: Symlink transparency in `get_diff`** ([ocaml/opam#6874](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6874)) by [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto): replaces `Unix.lstat` with `Unix.stat` so `file://` repositories with symlinks no longer fail
- **js_of_ocaml: Optional chaining in JS runtime** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2202](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2202)): `caml_wrap_exception` and `caml_current_dir` updated to use `?.` for more robust null-safety checks
group_summaries.group_overview:

group_summaries.activity_summary: dune internal digest refactoring, 3.22.1 backport management, Windows portability fixes, and opam incremental loading edge cases addressed across the group.
group_summaries.activity:
- **dune 3.22.1 release preparation**: [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) coordinated four backport merges ([ocaml/dune#13941](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13941), [ocaml/dune#13946](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13946), [ocaml/dune#13948](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13948), [ocaml/dune#13949](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13949)) targeting Windows and portability fixes, with the release draft PR [ocaml/dune#13947](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13947) in progress
- **dune digest and cache refactoring**: [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) decomposed `Cached_digest` into `Digest_result` and `Reduced_stats` modules, moved into `Fs_memo`, and relocated per-target digest storage into rule cache ([ocaml/dune#13956](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13956), [ocaml/dune#13957](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13957), [ocaml/dune#13958](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13958), [ocaml/dune#13959](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13959), [ocaml/dune#13945](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13945), [ocaml/dune#13938](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13938))
- **dune Windows and portability fixes**: Windows 7 compatibility restored in `time_stubs.c` by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) ([ocaml/dune#13905](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13905)), macOS clock fallback added ([ocaml/dune#13918](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13918)), and time stubs warning fixed ([ocaml/dune#13914](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13914))
- **dune package management fixes**: `OPAMSWITCH` in build env ([ocaml/dune#13888](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13888)), `.exe` handling on Windows ([ocaml/dune#13864](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13864)), stronger hash preference in `dune pkg lock` ([ocaml/dune#13901](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13901)), and relative path lock outside workspace ([ocaml/dune#13915](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13915)) all merged
- **dune CI improvements**: [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247) added a CI job for package dependency specification and lower bounds checking ([ocaml/dune#13844](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13844)); [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) proposed running reverse dependency checks in CI as part of release hardening ([ocaml/dune#13897](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13897))
- **dune upcoming features in progress**: [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) has open PRs for `DUNE_PROJECT_ROOT` env var ([ocaml/dune#13934](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13934)), improved mtime precision ([ocaml/dune#13962](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13962)), source directory dependency fix ([ocaml/dune#13966](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13966)), and directory diff in `_build` ([ocaml/dune#13968](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13968), [ocaml/dune#13970](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13970))
- **merlin indexer improvements in review**: LRU-based memory bounding ([ocaml/merlin#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)) and pointer-based index file references ([ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)) both under active review with design discussion on correctness and safety
- **odoc OCaml 5.5 tracking**: draft compat PR [ocaml/odoc#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406) added to opam-alpha-repository; tracking issue [ocaml/odoc#1401](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1401) updated
- **opam incremental loading fix in progress**: [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) has draft PR [ocaml/opam#6871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6871) to correct the diff-loading mechanism introduced in opam 2.5.0 that scans from the repository root rather than the `packages/` subdirectory
- **js_of_ocaml Windows CI instability**: two approaches (pinning dune 3.21.0 in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2203](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2203) and pinning setup-ocaml in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2205](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2205)) were attempted and reverted within the same day; the underlying cram test path issue on Cygwin remains unresolved
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:

- **dune 3.22.0 regression affects js_of_ocaml CI**: [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) attempted to pin dune to 3.21.0 ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2203](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2203)) due to a cram test regression in dune 3.22.0 on Windows/Cygwin; dune 3.22.1 ([ocaml/dune#13947](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13947)) is being prepared partly to address such portability issues
- **ocamlformat 0.29.0 impact**: [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) merged ocamlformat 0.29.0 ([ocaml/merlin#2047](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2047)), and the same upgrade created a user-reported conflict in [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) ([ocaml/odoc#1317](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1317)) where the new formatter changes indentation in odoc-style code blocks, prompting discussion of a possible `{@ocaml noformat[ ... ]}` syntax
- **[Charlène Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) active in both merlin and ocaml-lsp**: opened the LRU cache PR in [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) ([ocaml/merlin#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)) and the signature_help revert in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1598](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1598)) in the same week
- **[@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) active in both merlin and odoc**: provided correctness reviews on merlin indexer PRs ([ocaml/merlin#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050), [ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)) and maintains the OxCaml polymorphic parameter fix draft in odoc ([ocaml/odoc#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1404))
- **[Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) active in both dune and js_of_ocaml**: drove the OCaml 4.14 minimum bump and compat shim removal in dune ([ocaml/dune#13533](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13533)), while also providing feedback on Windows CI failures in js_of_ocaml ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2203](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2203))
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **OxCaml CI integration for dune** ([ocaml/dune#13895](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13895)): [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) proposed adding OxCaml to dune's CI matrix after a 3.22.0 regression ([ocaml/dune#13889](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13889)), prompting team discussion on integration scope and tooling
- **dune command retry when global lock held** ([ocaml/dune#13964](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13964)): [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) proposed retry support for concurrent dune invocations blocked by the global lock, improving UX for parallel build scenarios
- **Selective toplevel target building** ([ocaml/dune#13920](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13920)): [Mathieu Barbin](https://github.com/mbarbin) raised questions about building toplevel targets only within a package or under an enabling condition, touching on user-facing build scope control
- **opam incremental loading root scan bug** ([ocaml/opam#6872](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6872)): [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) filed a report that incremental repository loading in opam 2.5.0 scans from the repository root rather than `packages/`, causing missed packages on first load; related fix in draft PR [ocaml/opam#6871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6871)
- **merlin LRU implementation design** ([ocaml/merlin#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)): [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w), [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw), and [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) discussed trade-offs between pre-allocated arrays vs. dynamic allocation, initial hashtable sizing, and whether memory-size-weighted eviction is needed
- **Pointer index safety concerns** ([ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)): [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) raised risks of pointing indexes targeting removed or updated files, and of multiple pointers to the same target creating separate caches instead of sharing one
- **ocamlformat 0.29 and odoc code block indentation** ([ocaml/odoc#1317](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1317)): [Mathieu Barbin](https://github.com/mbarbin) reported widespread indentation changes across their codebase after upgrading ocamlformat; [Jules Aguillon](https://github.com/Julow) proposed a `{@ocaml noformat[ ... ]}` attribute to opt code blocks out of reformatting
- **Nushell quoting semantics in opam** ([ocaml/opam#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6873)): [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) and [@jneem](https://github.com/jneem) worked through Nushell-specific quoting behaviour, correct platform config paths, and boolean environment value handling across Nushell versions
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **OCaml 5.5 and OxCaml readiness** is a recurring theme across the group: [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) has a dedicated compat PR [ocaml/odoc#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406) and tracking issue [ocaml/odoc#1401](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1401); [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) has a large draft support PR [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195); [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) is tracking the OCaml 5.6 trunk ([ocaml/dune#13950](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13950))
