- **Wasm backend correctness** in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml): two targeted correctness fixes opened this week ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2207](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2207) empty closure identity, [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2208](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2208) stale global data flow), alongside the large Dynlink/toplevel PR [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187)
- **Indexer memory and storage efficiency** in [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin): both PRs this week ([ocaml/merlin#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050) LRU cache, [ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051) pointer-based indexes) target the indexer subsystem's memory footprint and on-disk overhead
- **dune diff and promote workflow expansion**: multiple merged PRs extended dune's diff/promote capabilities (directories [ocaml/dune#13880](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13880), path prefixes [ocaml/dune#13876](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13876), sandboxed corrections [ocaml/dune#13813](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13813)) with further PRs in progress, indicating a deliberate effort to make the diff/promote workflow more comprehensive
- **opam repository loading reliability**: incremental loading introduced in opam 2.5.0 continues to surface edge cases, with both [ocaml/opam#6871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6871) and [ocaml/opam#6872](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6872) addressing incorrect path handling during diff-based updates

group_summaries.brief_summary: 33 merges in oxcaml/oxcaml covering metaprogramming soundness, mode system expansion, backend fixes; Merlin gained -linscan support.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: New Gc.Memprof API, sound quote/splice kinding, mode annotations in quotes, new compiler flags, ARM64 improvements; Merlin ignores -linscan.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **`Gc.Memprof.participate_globally`** added to the standard library, enabling memory profiling participation across all domains; the existing `participate` function was renamed to `enlist` and the new function surfaces as `enlist_all_domains` in the public API ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5644](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5644))
- **Sound kinding of quotes and splices** — the metaprogramming system now enforces correct kinding rules at quote and splice boundaries, with `camlinternalQuote.mli` gaining a new `Modes` module (`legacy`, `of_string_list`) and `Type.arrow` accepting two extra `Modes.t` arguments ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5636](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5636))
- **Mode annotations under quotes** are now supported, allowing programmers to annotate modes inside quoted expressions; related changes in `Pat.constraint_` and `Type_constraint.constraint_` in `camlinternalQuote.mli` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5670](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5670))
- **New compiler flags `-requires-metaprogramming` and `-uses-metaprogramming`** added to `driver/main_args.mli`, along with a new `ui_requires_metaprogramming` field in `cmx_format.mli` unit info structures, enabling the compiler to track and enforce metaprogramming dependencies ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5544](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5544))
- **`eval` made a regular function** — the metaprogramming `eval` extension point was replaced with a standard function call, removing the special-case extension point syntax and simplifying the implementation across 66 files ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5657](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5657))
- **External modalities in `with`-bounds** — the type system now supports external modality constraints in `with`-bound expressions, expanding the expressiveness of the modes system ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5601](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5601))
- **`observing` mode renamed to `reading`** across the codebase and documentation, aligning the mode name with its intended semantics ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5712](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5712))
- **ARM64 instruction sizes derived robustly** — a comprehensive rework replaces fragile hardcoded sizes with derived measurements; `Instruction.max_displacement` added; `relax_poll`, `relax_allocation`, `relax_condbranch`, `relax_branch` separated as distinct functions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5499](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5499))
- **x86 LEA instruction used for add/sub with immediates** — the x86 backend now emits `lea` for additions and subtractions with immediate operands, improving code generation quality ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5695](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5695))
- **Register allocation interference edges** now added between multiple outputs of the same instruction, preventing incorrect register assignments for instructions that produce multiple results ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5706](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5706))
- **`~level` parameter removed** from the jkind comparison and violation API (`Jkind.compare`, `Jkind.check_sub`, etc.), simplifying call sites throughout the type checker ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5615](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5615))
- **`-linscan` flag silently ignored by Merlin** — added to Merlin's list of ignored OCaml compiler flags in `src/kernel/mconfig.ml`, preventing Merlin from erroring when projects pass this linear scan register allocator flag ([oxcaml/merlin#226](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/226))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was concentrated in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) (33 merges, ~30 new PRs opened) and [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) (1 PR merged); [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository), [oxcaml/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/js_of_ocaml), and [janestreet/opam-repository](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository) had no recorded activity
- The dominant theme in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) was **metaprogramming soundness** — four PRs merged fixing quote/splice kinding ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5636](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5636)), mode annotations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5670](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5670)), `eval` semantics ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5657](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5657)), and jkind annotation safety ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5685](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5685))
- **Mode system expansion** continued with the `observing`→`reading` rename ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5712](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5712)) merged and three new mode pairs opened: `write`/`writing` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608)), `corrupted`/`corruptable` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5713](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5713)), `poisoned`/`poisoning` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5666](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5666))
- **Flambda2 reaper reliability** improved with three bug fixes merged: exception parameter deletion ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5563](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5563)), uses/sources deadlock ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5648](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5648)), and stamp debug printing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5700](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5700))
- **5.4 upstream merge** is in sustained progress across stdlib, runtime, ocamltest, and debugger subsystems ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5692](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5692), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5693](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5693), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5598](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5598), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5711](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5711), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5717](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5717))
- **Backend code quality**: ARM64 instruction size derivation reworked ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5499](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5499)), x86 LEA emission improved ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5695](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5695)), register allocation interference edges fixed ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5706](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5706)), CFG merge block predecessors corrected ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5708](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5708))
- 25 contributors active across [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) in code, reviews, and discussions; [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) and [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) led by volume of merged work
group_summaries.activity_summary: Dozens of PRs opened in oxcaml/oxcaml spanning metaprogramming staging, new modes, codegen improvements, and 5.4 upstream merge work.
group_summaries.activity:
- **Newly opened metaprogramming PRs** extend quote/splice soundness: sound moding of quotes/splices by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5697](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5697)), quotes of expansive expressions at mode `once` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5698](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5698)), top-level expressions at mode `once` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5715](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5715)), and environment stage tracking with GADT interaction tests ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5641](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5641))
- **New codegen and compiler infrastructure PRs opened**: CFG SSA reconstruction by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5722](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5722)), new Lfor compilation strategy by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5728](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5728)), `-thunkify-compilation-unit-initialization` flag by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5720](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5720)), static modules by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5724](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5724)), codegen examples for materialized bits ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5727](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5727))
- **5.4 upstream merge work** continues: labeled tuples support ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5692](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5692)), Stdlib iarray ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5693](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5693)), `make install` for OxCaml 5.4 ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5717](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5717)), testsuite conflicts ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5711](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5711)), and runtime merge ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5598](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5598)); completed merges include stdlib conflicts ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5616](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5616)), ocamltest ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5605](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5605)), debugger ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5617](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5617)), and miscellany ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5729](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5729))
- **Reaper correctness**: three fixes merged by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) — exception parameter deletion bug ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5563](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5563)), deadlock between uses and sources computation with local fields ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5648](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5648)), and value slot stamp printing suppression ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5700](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5700)); reaper testcase for [oxcaml/oxcaml#5633](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5633) added ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5682](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5682)) and coaccessor/coconstructor renames ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5681](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5681))
- **Flambda2 continuation lifting** improved: fix preventing lifting of continuations that may refer to non-lifted continuations by [Guillaume Bury](https://github.com/Gbury) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5689](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5689)), and a fix for unbound variables in lifted constants by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5725](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5725)), superseding the earlier approach in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5719](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5719)
- **n-way join improvements**: missing-cmx variable scoping fix by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5679](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5679)), follow-on PR using untagged const false for nullability detection ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5718](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5718)) opened; related issue [oxcaml/oxcaml#5721](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5721) reporting missing CSE with n-way join
- **Sort and jkind type system cleanup**: generic sort variable simplification by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5704](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5704)), `Jkind.Sort.new_var` return type corrected ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5658](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5658)), parameter vs return mode fix ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5596](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5596))
- **Slambda fracturing** initial implementation merged — adds the simplest slambda fracturing pass by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5544](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5544))
- **CI and build fixes**: dune pinned to 3.20.2 in CI ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5684](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5684)), Dune 3.22 compatibility fix ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5686](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5686)), missing compiler-libs-installation.sexp entries added ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5709](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5709)), macOS compilation fix opened by [Amethyst Shen](https://github.com/Amekagura) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5714](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5714))
- **Ongoing abstract kinds** work by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin): `with kind_` constraints part 3 ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5629](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5629)) and tests for `is_really_poly` in typecore ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5730](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5730))
- **Ongoing mode polymorphism** work by [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges): functional mode polymorphism ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5483](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5483)), alloc mode ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5482](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5482)), copy operation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5481](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5481)), normal form morphisms ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5453](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5453)), and printing of polymorphic mode variables ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5488](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5488))
- **`Multicore` module domain lifetime fix** merged — joining domains could block indefinitely waiting for the ticker thread; `domain.mli` alert text updated by [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5638](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5638))
- **oxcaml/merlin**: [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened, authored, and merged [oxcaml/merlin#226](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/226), adding `-linscan` to the ignored compiler flags list in `src/kernel/mconfig.ml`
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **oxcaml/oxcaml → oxcaml/merlin**: The `-linscan` linear scan register allocator flag (used in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) backend work) was missing from Merlin's ignored flags list, causing spurious errors; [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) fixed this in [oxcaml/merlin#226](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/226) — [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) also merged the jkind error newline fix in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5578](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5578), reflecting active engagement across both repositories
- **Dune build system**: CI dune pin to 3.20.2 ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5684](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5684)) and Dune 3.22 compatibility fix ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5686](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5686)) in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) indicate build toolchain changes that could affect downstream repositories dependent on the same dune version
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Zero-alloc function arguments** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5493](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5493)) received the most review activity this week — [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) posted extensive comments covering `Tpoly` node handling, `is_really_poly` correctness, `filter_arrow` interactions, the distinction between `Zero_alloc.check option` and `Zero_alloc.t`, and `translcore` complications; the PR by [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian) remains open pending resolution
- **`unique implies uncontended`** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5716](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5716)) prompted theoretical discussion between [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) and [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25) on lattice structure for the modes system — specifically whether `aliased => contended` should be modeled as a general mechanism, how normal forms interact with fixpoints in mode operators, and whether this belongs as a jkind axis
- **Tick thread in runtime** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5349](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5349)) continued review by [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) covering threading model correctness, non-blocking syscall safety, locking discipline, and interval timer accuracy for preemptive scheduling
- **Preemption** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#4881](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4881)) saw discussion between [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) and [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) on GC-safe stack frame traversal during preemption
- **`raise` instead of dropping jkind annotations in quotes** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5674](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5674)) discussion on how the compiler should handle jkind annotations inside quoted expressions, leading to the merged fix in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5685](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5685)
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Metaprogramming soundness hardening** is a clear focus: four PRs merged fixing quote/splice kinding ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5636](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5636)), mode annotations in quotes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5670](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5670)), `eval` semantics ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5657](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5657)), and jkind annotation safety ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5685](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5685)), with four more PRs opened extending the same area ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5697](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5697), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5698](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5698), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5715](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5715), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5641](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5641))
- **Mode system vocabulary expansion**: three new mode pairs introduced — `write`/`writing` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608)), `corrupted`/`corruptable` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5713](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5713)), `poisoned`/`poisoning` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5666](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5666)) — on top of the `observing`→`reading` rename ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5712](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5712)); the mode lattice is actively evolving with theoretical debate on implications such as `unique implies uncontended` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5716](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5716))
- **Backend code quality sustained attention**: ARM64 instruction size derivation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5499](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5499)), x86 LEA usage ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5695](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5695)), register allocation interference ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5706](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5706)), CFG SSA reconstruction ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5722](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5722)), x86 peephole optimizations ongoing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5639](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5639)), and optimization fuel bisection ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5651](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5651))
- **Flambda2 reaper reliability**: three fixes merged in one week ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5563](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5563), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5648](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5648), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5700](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5700)) alongside continuation lifting fixes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5689](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5689), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5725](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5725)), suggesting the reaper is in active hardening phase
- **5.4 upstream merge** in sustained progress with dedicated contributors ([David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js), [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon), [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes)) working through stdlib, runtime, toplevel, ocamltest, and debugger subsystems in parallel ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5692](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5692), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5693](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5693), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5598](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5598), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5711](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5711), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5717](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5717))
- **Merlin compiler flag synchronization**: the `-linscan` fix ([oxcaml/merlin#226](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/226)) highlights a recurring maintenance need to keep Merlin's ignored compiler flags list current with upstream OCaml compiler flags, with intent to add systematic testing to catch similar gaps

group_summaries.brief_summary: 10 PRs merged in ocaml/ocaml: GC ephemeron revert/re-fix, POWER CFI, type system improvements, and testsuite speedups.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: POWER CFI support, modular explicit warnings, class type improvements, Dynarray iarray conversions, and x86 prefetchw simplification.
group_summaries.new_features:
- [ocaml/ocaml#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14482): DWARF CFI support for the POWER backend, bringing backtraces and GDB debugger/profiler support to the last native backend missing them. By [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist), reviewed by [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14667](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14667): Application warnings for modular explicit (module-dependent) functions — partial application, over-application, and unused argument warnings now enabled for `Tfunctor` types. By [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), reviewed by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14680](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14680): Class type system improvements (rebase of [ocaml/ocaml#12150](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12150) by [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25)) — fixes long-standing issues in the OO type system. Rebased and merged by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14394](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14394): Refactor `type_let` into `type_let_nonrec` and `type_let_rec` — prerequisite for future improvements to recursive-binding typing. By [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob), reviewed by [Samuel Vivien](https://github.com/samsa1).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14693](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14693): `Dynarray.{of,to}_iarray` — adds conversions between `Dynarray` and `iarray` (immutable arrays) with a single-copy implementation. By [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole), approved by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14665](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14665): Always emit `prefetchw` on x86/x86_64, removing the need for the `-mprfchw` compiler flag. By [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA).
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was concentrated in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml); [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) had no recorded activity.
- 10 PRs merged in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) with 33 files changed (1018 insertions, 393 deletions).
- Key themes: GC correctness (ephemeron race condition diagnosed, reverted via [ocaml/ocaml#14682](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14682), and re-attempted via [ocaml/ocaml#14688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14688)), type-system infrastructure, and architecture backend completeness.
- 17+ contributors active; [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) authored or co-reviewed 5 merged PRs.
- Notable open work: GC for continuations ([ocaml/ocaml#14580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14580)) and a security-relevant Bigarray overflow fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14691](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14691)) pending review.
group_summaries.activity_summary: 13 PRs merged or closed in ocaml/ocaml; active GC debugging around ephemeron race conditions and testsuite performance improvements.
group_summaries.activity:
**Merged PRs:**
- [ocaml/ocaml#14680](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14680): Class type system improvements (rebase of [ocaml/ocaml#12150](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12150)) — 359 additions, 189 deletions. By [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron).

- [ocaml/ocaml#14683](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14683): Fix memory-model tests default test size. By [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche).

- [ocaml/ocaml#14669](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14669): Allow `Config.is_official_release` to change without bootstrap. By [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14662](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14662): Correct order of compiler flags in ocamltest. By [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14678](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14678): Fix test for [ocaml/ocaml#14380](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14380) (reserved_header_bits config variable). By [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez).

**Open PRs with notable activity:**
- [ocaml/ocaml#14688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14688): Revised ephemeron optimization (without the race condition) — under review.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14580): GC for continuations (draft) — infrastructure for automatically finalising dropped effects continuations. By [KC Sivaramakrishnan](https://github.com/kayceesrk).

group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) had no recorded activity this week; all cross-repository coordination was informal.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668): Discussion on adding extension point placeholders to more AST positions in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) for ppxlib compatibility — an ongoing coordination point between the compiler and the ppxlib project.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14673](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14673): Ecosystem testing of [ocaml/ocaml#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11648) (`keep-expansion`) against external packages (ppxlib, base_quickcheck) revealed integration issues that need resolution before merger.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Minor heap size defaults** ([ocaml/ocaml#14634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14634)): [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) argues the 256KW (2MiB) default, unchanged since 2011, is too conservative for modern hardware; benchmarks show up to 3x speedup with `s=32M` on allocation-heavy programs. [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) supports increasing the default (Jane Street has used 8MiB since 2017) but cautions against over-interpreting synthetic benchmarks.
- **GC for continuations** ([ocaml/ocaml#14580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14580)): [KC Sivaramakrishnan](https://github.com/kayceesrk) proposes automatically finalising dropped effects continuations for resource cleanup; debate about whether this encourages treating `Fun.protect` finalizers as GC finalizers (~2% overhead on chameneos benchmark).
- **Extension points in the OCaml AST for ppxlib** ([ocaml/ocaml#14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668)): [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) proposes adding extension point placeholders to more AST positions (especially `type_declaration`) so ppxlib can encode new language features without ppx breakage.
- **Ephemeron race condition** ([ocaml/ocaml#14679](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14679)): [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) diagnosed a race in [ocaml/ocaml#14657](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14657) where `num_domains_to_mark` was decremented before `ephe_next_round()` was called, causing major GC deadlock in multi-domain programs. The PR was reverted ([ocaml/ocaml#14682](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14682)) and a fixed version ([ocaml/ocaml#14688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14688)) proposed.
- **`keep-expansion` type-checker PR** ([ocaml/ocaml#14673](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14673)): Ecosystem testing of [ocaml/ocaml#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11648) ([Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue)'s multi-year type-checker correctness fix) revealed an "Unbound record field" in ppxlib and a `Ctype.Unify` exception in base_quickcheck.
- **Segfaults in cleanup-at-exit mode** ([ocaml/ocaml#14594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14594)): Ongoing debate on whether `caml_shutdown` should cancel unjoined domains; [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) argues the cleanup-at-exit mode has both false positives and false negatives and should be deleted.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Ephemeron performance and correctness**: A full cycle of merge, revert ([ocaml/ocaml#14682](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14682)), and re-attempt ([ocaml/ocaml#14688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14688)) of ephemeron GC optimization illustrates the difficulty of concurrent GC modifications; the fix targets a quadratic blowup affecting the Rocq proof assistant's evar map use case.
- **Type-checker infrastructure overhaul**: Multiple interrelated PRs ([ocaml/ocaml#14394](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14394), [ocaml/ocaml#14538](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14538), [ocaml/ocaml#14517](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14517)) by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) systematically refactor `type_let` and `type_approx` to enable correct handling of polymorphic recursive bindings — groundwork for fixing [ocaml/ocaml#14253](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14253).
- **`keep-expansion` type correctness** ([ocaml/ocaml#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11648) / [ocaml/ocaml#14673](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14673)): [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue)'s multi-year effort to fix principality and non-termination bugs via `Texpand` type nodes is approaching merger, with ecosystem testing now revealing integration issues in ppxlib and base_quickcheck.
- **CI robustness and testsuite speed**: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) merged multiple PRs reducing testsuite runtime ([ocaml/ocaml#14681](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14681): lazy5.ml 10s→0.1s; [ocaml/ocaml#14683](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14683): memory-model tests 7s→2s).
- **Architecture backend completeness**: POWER CFI support ([ocaml/ocaml#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14482)) completes native debugger support across all supported architectures; s390x CFI fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14500)) also pending.
- **ppxlib/compiler interface evolution**: Discussion on [ocaml/ocaml#14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668) reflects ongoing coordination between ppxlib (moving to a frozen AST model) and the compiler team.

group_summaries.brief_summary: Quiet week across the multicore group; only eio saw activity, with discussion of a race condition in Eio.Path.load.
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was limited to [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio); all other repositories (domainslib, ocaml-uring, meio, saturn, eio-trace, picos, kcas, ocaml-iomux) had no recorded activity.
- The sole active thread was [ocaml-multicore/eio#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835), a bug report on a race condition in `Eio.Path.load` that also surfaces as a functional failure when reading `/proc` virtual files.
group_summaries.activity_summary: Discussion continued on ocaml-multicore/eio#835, a race condition in Eio.Path.load that causes silent data loss and breaks /proc file reads.
group_summaries.activity:
- [ocaml-multicore/eio#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835) received a follow-up comment during the week adding background reading on `/proc` file semantics, keeping the discussion active ahead of a potential fix.
- The bug arises because `Eio.Path.load` uses the result of `stat` to size its read buffer: if the file shrinks between `stat` and `read`, truncated data is returned silently; if it grows, data beyond the initial allocation is lost.
- `/proc` virtual files report size 0 at `stat` time, causing `Eio.Path.load` to return an empty string for files such as `/proc/sys/fs/file-nr`.
- The proposed fix is to treat the `stat` size only as an initial buffer-sizing hint and otherwise read to completion, matching the behaviour of `Eio.Path.with_open_in path Eio.Flow.read_all`.
group_summaries.notable_discussions: - [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) filed and developed [ocaml-multicore/eio#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835), providing a reproducer, a root-cause analysis of the stat-before-read race, and a concrete fix strategy; a follow-up comment during the week added further context on `/proc` file behaviour.

weekly_summary.brief_summary: OCaml 5.5 alpha3 landed; OxCaml saw 33 merges on metaprogramming and modes; dune gained directory diffing and promote improvements.
weekly_summary.new_features_summary: POWER CFI support, dune directory diffing and promote prefix, opam Nushell support, OxCaml Gc.Memprof API, new ocaml.org tutorials with llms.txt.
weekly_summary.new_features:
**Compiler (ocaml/ocaml)**
- __RUMINANT:core__ **DWARF CFI support for the POWER backend** merged, bringing backtrace and GDB debugger/profiler support to the last native backend missing it ([ocaml/ocaml#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14482)) by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist), reviewed by [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Application warnings for modular explicit functions** — partial application, over-application, and unused argument warnings now enabled for `Tfunctor` types ([ocaml/ocaml#14667](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14667)) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Class type system improvements** merged (rebase of [ocaml/ocaml#12150](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12150) by [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25)), fixing long-standing OO type system issues ([ocaml/ocaml#14680](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14680)) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`Dynarray.{of,to}_iarray`** adds conversions between `Dynarray` and `iarray` (immutable arrays) with a single-copy implementation ([ocaml/ocaml#14693](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14693)) by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`prefetchw` always emitted on x86/x86_64**, removing the need for the `-mprfchw` compiler flag ([ocaml/ocaml#14665](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14665)) by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`type_let` refactored** into `type_let_nonrec` and `type_let_rec`, a prerequisite for future improvements to recursive-binding typing ([ocaml/ocaml#14394](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14394)) by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob)

**OxCaml (oxcaml/oxcaml)**
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`Gc.Memprof.participate_globally`** added to the standard library, enabling memory profiling participation across all domains; `participate` renamed to `enlist` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5644](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5644))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Sound kinding of quotes and splices** — the metaprogramming system now enforces correct kinding rules at quote and splice boundaries, with new `Modes` module in `camlinternalQuote.mli` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5636](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5636))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Mode annotations under quotes** now supported, allowing mode annotations inside quoted expressions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5670](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5670))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **New compiler flags `-requires-metaprogramming` and `-uses-metaprogramming`** added, enabling the compiler to track and enforce metaprogramming dependencies ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5544](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5544))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`eval` made a regular function** — the metaprogramming `eval` extension point replaced with a standard function call, simplifying implementation across 66 files ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5657](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5657))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **External modalities in `with`-bounds** — the type system now supports external modality constraints in `with`-bound expressions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5601](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5601))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`observing` mode renamed to `reading`** across the codebase and documentation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5712](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5712))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **ARM64 instruction sizes derived robustly** — replaces fragile hardcoded sizes with derived measurements; `relax_poll`, `relax_allocation`, `relax_condbranch`, `relax_branch` separated as distinct functions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5499](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5499))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **x86 LEA instruction used for add/sub with immediates**, improving code generation quality ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5695](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5695))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Register allocation interference edges** now added between multiple outputs of the same instruction ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5706](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5706))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`-linscan` flag silently ignored by Merlin**, preventing errors when projects pass this flag ([oxcaml/merlin#226](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/226)) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)

**Build Tools (dune, opam, js_of_ocaml)**
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **dune directory diffing** in `diff` actions now supports diffing entire directories (requires `lang dune 3.23`), closing a request open since 2018 ([ocaml/dune#13880](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13880))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **dune promote prefix targeting** — `dune promote` can now promote all paths sharing a given prefix ([ocaml/dune#13876](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13876))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **dune sandboxed rule corrections** — sandboxed actions can produce promotable `.corrected` files when `(corrections produce)` is set ([ocaml/dune#13813](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13813))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **`dune clean` path arguments** — `dune clean` now accepts path arguments to remove specific targets from `_build` ([ocaml/dune#13875](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13875))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **dune minimum OCaml version raised to 4.14** ([ocaml/dune#13533](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13533)), dropping accumulated compatibility shims
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **opam Nushell shell support** via JSON-based environment loading opened by [@jneem](https://github.com/jneem) ([ocaml/opam#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6873))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **opam symlink transparency in `get_diff`** ([ocaml/opam#6874](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6874)) by [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto): `file://` repositories with symlinks no longer fail
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **js_of_ocaml optional chaining in JS runtime** — `caml_wrap_exception` and `caml_current_dir` updated to use `?.` for more robust null-safety checks ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2202](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2202))

weekly_summary.group_overview:
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **OxCaml** was the highest-volume group with 33 merges in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml), focused on **metaprogramming soundness** (4 PRs fixing quote/splice kinding, `eval` semantics, and jkind annotations), **mode system expansion** (`observing`→`reading` rename plus three new mode pairs), Flambda2 reaper correctness, and sustained **5.4 upstream merge** progress
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Core compiler** merged 10 PRs in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml): **POWER CFI support** completes native debugger coverage across all backends, **modular explicit warnings** and **class type improvements** extend the type system, and a **GC ephemeron race condition** was diagnosed, reverted ([ocaml/ocaml#14682](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14682)), and re-attempted ([ocaml/ocaml#14688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14688))

weekly_summary.activity:
**Core compiler activity ([ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml))**
- __RUMINANT:core__ **GC ephemeron fix cycle**: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) diagnosed a race in [ocaml/ocaml#14657](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14657) where `num_domains_to_mark` was decremented before `ephe_next_round()`, causing major GC deadlock in multi-domain programs; the PR was reverted ([ocaml/ocaml#14682](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14682)) and a fixed version ([ocaml/ocaml#14688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14688)) submitted; the underlying fix targets a quadratic blowup affecting Rocq's evar map use case
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Testsuite speed improvements**: `lazy/lazy5.ml` reduced from 10s to 0.1s ([ocaml/ocaml#14681](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14681)) and memory-model tests from 7s to 2s ([ocaml/ocaml#14683](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14683)), both by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`Config.is_official_release`** can now change without bootstrap ([ocaml/ocaml#14669](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14669)) and ocamltest compiler flag order corrected ([ocaml/ocaml#14662](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14662)), both by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)

**OxCaml activity ([oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml))**
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Flambda2 reaper correctness**: three fixes by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) — exception parameter deletion bug ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5563](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5563)), deadlock between uses and sources computation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5648](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5648)), and stamp debug printing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5700](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5700))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Flambda2 continuation lifting**: fix for lifting continuations that may refer to non-lifted continuations by [Guillaume Bury](https://github.com/Gbury) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5689](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5689)); unbound variables in lifted constants fix by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5725](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5725))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **5.4 upstream merge** sustained across stdlib, runtime, ocamltest, and debugger subsystems: labeled tuples ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5692](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5692)), Stdlib iarray ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5693](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5693)), runtime ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5598](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5598)), testsuite conflicts ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5711](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5711)), `make install` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5717](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5717)); completed: stdlib conflicts ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5616](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5616)), ocamltest ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5605](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5605)), debugger ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5617](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5617))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Mode system and jkind cleanups**: `~level` parameter removed from jkind comparison API ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5615](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5615)); sort variable simplification by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5704](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5704)); three new mode pairs opened: `write`/`writing` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608)), `corrupted`/`corruptable` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5713](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5713)), `poisoned`/`poisoning` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5666](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5666))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **n-way join improvements**: missing-cmx variable scoping fix by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5679](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5679)); CSE missing with n-way join issue filed ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5721](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5721))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Multicore domain lifetime fix** — joining domains could block indefinitely waiting for the ticker thread; `domain.mli` alert text updated by [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5638](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5638))

- __RUMINANT:tools__ __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **OxCaml CI integration for dune**: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) proposed adding OxCaml to dune's CI matrix ([ocaml/dune#13895](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13895)) after a 3.22.0 regression ([ocaml/dune#13889](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13889)); the [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) CI simultaneously pinned dune to 3.20.2 ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5684](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5684)) and applied a 3.22 compatibility fix ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5686](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5686))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **dune 3.22.0 regression in js_of_ocaml**: [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) attempted to pin dune to 3.21.0 ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2203](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2203)) due to a cram test regression on Windows/Cygwin; dune 3.22.1 ([ocaml/dune#13947](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13947)) is being prepared partly to address these portability issues
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **oxcaml/oxcaml → oxcaml/merlin**: the `-linscan` register allocator flag used in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) was missing from Merlin's ignored flags list; [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) fixed this in [oxcaml/merlin#226](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/226), reflecting the need to keep Merlin synchronized with compiler flags
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **ocamlformat 0.29.0 impact**: [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) merged the ocamlformat 0.29.0 upgrade ([ocaml/merlin#2047](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2047)); the same upgrade created indentation conflicts in [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) code blocks ([ocaml/odoc#1317](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1317)), prompting discussion of a `{@ocaml noformat[ ... ]}` opt-out syntax
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Dune 3.22.0 cross-repo coordination**: the ocamlformat Dune 3.22 constraint fix in [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29568](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29568)) and the Dune 3.22.0 announcement on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3570](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3570)) reflect downstream coordination triggered by the same upstream release
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **OCaml 5.5 readiness spans all groups**: ppxlib 0.38.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29563](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29563)), lsp 1.26.0 preview ([ocaml/opam-repository#29565](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29565)), odoc compat draft ([ocaml/odoc#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406)), js_of_ocaml support PR ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195)), and dune 5.6 trunk tracking ([ocaml/dune#13950](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13950)) are all progressing in parallel
- __RUMINANT:core__ **ppxlib/compiler interface coordination**: ecosystem testing of the `keep-expansion` PR ([ocaml/ocaml#14673](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14673)) against external packages revealed a ppxlib "Unbound record field" and a `Ctype.Unify` exception in base_quickcheck; separately, [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) proposed adding extension point placeholders to more AST positions ([ocaml/ocaml#14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668)) to enable ppxlib's frozen AST model

weekly_summary.notable_discussions:
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Minor heap size defaults** ([ocaml/ocaml#14634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14634)): [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) argues the 256KW (2MiB) default, unchanged since 2011, is too conservative for modern hardware; benchmarks show up to 3x speedup with `s=32M` on allocation-heavy programs. [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) supports increasing the default (Jane Street has used 8MiB since 2017) but cautions against over-interpreting synthetic benchmarks
- __RUMINANT:core__ **GC for continuations** ([ocaml/ocaml#14580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14580)): [KC Sivaramakrishnan](https://github.com/kayceesrk) proposes automatically finalising dropped effects continuations for resource cleanup; debate about whether this encourages treating `Fun.protect` finalizers as GC finalizers (~2% overhead on chameneos benchmark)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Segfaults in cleanup-at-exit mode** ([ocaml/ocaml#14594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14594)): [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) argues the cleanup-at-exit mode has both false positives and false negatives and should be deleted
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Zero-alloc function arguments** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5493](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5493)): [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) posted extensive comments covering `Tpoly` node handling, `is_really_poly` correctness, `filter_arrow` interactions, and `translcore` complications; the PR by [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian) remains open
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`unique implies uncontended`** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5716](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5716)): [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) and [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25) discussed lattice structure for the modes system — specifically whether `aliased => contended` should be a general mechanism and how normal forms interact with fixpoints in mode operators
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Tick thread in runtime** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5349](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5349)): [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) reviewed threading model correctness, non-blocking syscall safety, and interval timer accuracy for preemptive scheduling
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **OxCaml CI integration for dune** ([ocaml/dune#13895](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13895)): [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) proposed adding OxCaml to dune's CI matrix after a 3.22.0 regression, prompting discussion on integration scope
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **opam incremental loading root scan bug** ([ocaml/opam#6872](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6872)): [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) filed a report that incremental repository loading in opam 2.5.0 scans from the repository root rather than `packages/`, causing missed packages on first load; fix in draft PR [ocaml/opam#6871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6871)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **merlin LRU implementation design** ([ocaml/merlin#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)): [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w), [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw), and [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) discussed pre-allocated arrays vs. dynamic allocation and memory-size-weighted eviction trade-offs
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Windows MSYS2 UCRT64 migration** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29578](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29578)): [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto) proposed conditional depexts for MINGW64 vs UCRT64 as MSYS2 deprecated MINGW64 in March 2026; MSVC builds incorrectly pulling in mingw dependencies also reported ([ocaml/opam-repository#29609](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29609))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **`Eio.Path.load` race condition** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835)): [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) provided a root-cause analysis of the `stat`-before-`read` race causing silent data loss, with a fix strategy of treating `stat` size only as a buffer-sizing hint

weekly_summary.emerging_trends:
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Metaprogramming soundness hardening** continues as a concentrated focus: 4 PRs merged fixing quote/splice kinding ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5636](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5636)), mode annotations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5670](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5670)), `eval` semantics ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5657](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5657)), and jkind annotation safety ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5685](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5685)), with 4 more PRs opened extending the same area ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5697](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5697), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5698](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5698), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5715](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5715), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5641](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5641))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Mode system vocabulary expansion**: three new mode pairs introduced — `write`/`writing` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608)), `corrupted`/`corruptable` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5713](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5713)), `poisoned`/`poisoning` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5666](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5666)) — on top of the `observing`→`reading` rename ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5712](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5712)); theoretical debate on lattice structure ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5716](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5716)) suggests the mode system is still in active design
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **OCaml 5.5 readiness accelerating** ahead of a planned April beta: the third alpha ([ocaml/opam-repository#29600](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29600)) landed alongside compatibility updates for ppxlib, utop, lsp, core, mdx, and ortac; tools groups are tracking 5.5 in odoc, js_of_ocaml, and dune in parallel
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **LLM-oriented OCaml content** emerging on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org): the `llms.txt` file ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3579](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3579)) and *OCaml for LLM Coding Agents* tutorial ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3584)) represent a new explicit focus on AI coding tool users
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Platform portability pressure across the toolchain**: Windows 7 compatibility in dune ([ocaml/dune#13905](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13905)), MSYS2 UCRT64 migration in opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#29578](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29578)), Cygwin cram test failures in js_of_ocaml ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2203](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2203)), and Nushell support in opam ([ocaml/opam#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6873)) all surfaced independently in the same week
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Type-checker infrastructure overhaul**: multiple interrelated PRs by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) ([ocaml/ocaml#14394](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14394), [ocaml/ocaml#14538](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14538), [ocaml/ocaml#14517](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14517)) systematically refactor `type_let` and `type_approx` to enable correct handling of polymorphic recursive bindings, as groundwork for fixing [ocaml/ocaml#14253](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14253)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Architecture backend completeness**: POWER CFI ([ocaml/ocaml#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14482)) completes native debugger support across all supported architectures; s390x CFI fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14500)) also pending
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **dune diff/promote workflow expansion**: directory diffing ([ocaml/dune#13880](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13880)), prefix promotion ([ocaml/dune#13876](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13876)), sandboxed corrections ([ocaml/dune#13813](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13813)), and directory diff in `_build` ([ocaml/dune#13968](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13968)) all merged or in progress, indicating a deliberate effort to make the diff/promote workflow more comprehensive
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Flambda2 reaper reliability**: three fixes merged in one week ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5563](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5563), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5648](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5648), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5700](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5700)) alongside continuation lifting fixes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5689](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5689), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5725](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5725)) suggests the reaper is in an active hardening phase
